<!-- Generated by spec-lite | skill: feature | date: 2026-06-28 -->

# Feature: Test suite overhaul

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-006
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan.md`

Replace the current false-green test suite with a trustworthy pytest-based suite.
Today `unittest discover` finds **0 tests** (files are named `*_tests.py` while the
default pattern is `test*.py`), pytest isn't installed, and several tests are
hollow (`asyncio.sleep(0)` left unawaited; mislabeled test classes). This feature
establishes pytest + pytest-asyncio, fixtures for executor/loop isolation, real
coverage of every code feature including the confirmed regressions, and a coverage
gate.

Business value: the owner explicitly wants solid unit-test coverage. For "central
crucial infrastructure," tests are the safety net for the refactor — and right now
the net has no rope in it.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

Not applicable.

## 3. Files

- `tests/conftest.py` — fixtures: per-test reset of executor module state (`shutdown()` + clear singletons), helpers for "with running loop" vs "no loop", a sample importable module for process-pool qualname resolution.
- `tests/_picklable_targets.py` — module-level functions/classes used by process-pool tests (must be importable, not defined in `__main__`).
- `tests/test_execution_core.py` — FEAT-001 coverage.
- `tests/test_functions.py` — FEAT-002 coverage.
- `tests/test_classes.py` — FEAT-003 coverage.
- `tests/test_properties.py` — FEAT-004 coverage.
- `tests/test_config_lifecycle.py` — config + shutdown + cross-loop regression.
- **Delete/replace**: all legacy `tests/*_tests.py`, `tests/testdata.py`, `tests/samples.py` (samples migrate to docs in FEAT-007; keep only if still referenced).
- `pyproject.toml` — `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` and `[tool.coverage.*]` (config wired in FEAT-005; threshold set here).

## 4. Dependencies

- **FEAT-001..004** (required): tests target the implemented APIs. Practically, each code feature should land its own tests as it is implemented; this spec is the consolidated plan and the home for the gate + isolation fixtures + cross-cutting regression tests.
- **FEAT-005** (required for the runner/CI wiring; this feature sets the threshold value).

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: pytest infrastructure, fixtures, and isolation

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Add `[tool.pytest.ini_options]`: `asyncio_mode = "auto"` (pytest-asyncio), `testpaths = ["tests"]`, default discovery (`test_*.py`).
  - `tests/conftest.py`: an autouse fixture that, before/after each test, resets `unblock._executors` module state (calls `shutdown()` and clears `_thread_pool`/`_process_pool`/`_is_shutdown`) so tests don't leak pools into each other.
  - Helpers/fixtures to run code both "inside a running loop" and "with no running loop" to exercise the duality.
  - `tests/_picklable_targets.py` with module-level sync functions and a sample class for process-pool tests.
  - Remove legacy `*_tests.py` files and the hollow tests.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: This task *is* test infra; validate by `pytest --collect-only` showing tests are discovered with default settings (the core regression: discovery must not be 0).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Contributor note: how to run `pytest`, `pytest --cov`.
- **Verify**: `pytest --collect-only` lists the new tests; `pytest -q` runs them.

### TASK-002: Function & method asyncification tests (FEAT-002)

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `tests/test_functions.py` cover: bare/parameterized/direct-call forms; duality (started Future under a running loop vs coroutine with no loop); method-descriptor handling; coroutine-function passthrough (identity); raise-on-misuse (`asyncify(42)` → `UnblockError`); `include=` on a callable → `UnblockError`; exception propagation from the worker; cancellation semantics (started thread work not force-killed) asserted and documented.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: (the implementation *is* the tests).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: N/A (covered by FEAT-007 doc examples).
- **Verify**: `pytest tests/test_functions.py -q` passes.

### TASK-003: Process-pool tests (trampoline, fail-fast, propagation)

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `tests/test_functions.py` (or a dedicated `tests/test_process_pool.py`), using `tests/_picklable_targets.py`: decorator form `@asyncify(executor="process")` returns correct result; direct-call form works; closure/lambda raises `UnblockError` at decoration time; worker-process exception propagates; result correctness across the process boundary. Mark these to also run on the Windows CI runner (spawn).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: (implementation is the tests).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: N/A.
- **Verify**: `pytest -k process -q` passes on Linux and Windows runners.

### TASK-004: Class decorator + mixin tests (FEAT-003)

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `tests/test_classes.py`: default selection (public instance methods only; dunder/private/static/class/async skipped); `include`/`exclude` behavior and mutual-exclusion error; nonexistent/invalid name error; build-once identity (`obj.method is obj.method` — wrapper stable, not re-created per access); already-async methods untouched; `AsyncMixin` wrapper pattern leaves the base class unmodified; `executor="process"` via class kwarg.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: (implementation is the tests).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: N/A.
- **Verify**: `pytest tests/test_classes.py -q` passes, including the build-once identity assertion.

### TASK-005: Iterator + context-manager tests (FEAT-003)

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `tests/test_classes.py`: `async for` over an auto-detected/`AsyncIterMixin` class yields the sync sequence and ends via `StopAsyncIteration`; **non-blocking assertion** — `__enter__`/`__exit__` run off the loop thread (record the executing thread and assert it is not the main/loop thread); `aclose` awaited when present; sync `close` offloaded when no `aclose`; `call_close_on_exit=False` skips extra close but still runs `__exit__`; combined ctx+iter via `AsyncContextIterMixin`; block exceptions routed through `__exit__`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: (implementation is the tests).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: N/A.
- **Verify**: `pytest -k "iter or context" -q` passes, including the off-the-loop-thread assertion.

### TASK-006: Properties tests (FEAT-004)

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `tests/test_properties.py`: `async_property` reflects state changes (no caching); `async_cached_property` caches (value persists after state change); **compute-once under concurrency** — `asyncio.gather` of N concurrent accesses invokes the getter exactly once (assert call counter == 1); per-instance isolation; getter exception propagates; class-level access returns the descriptor.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: (implementation is the tests).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: N/A.
- **Verify**: `pytest tests/test_properties.py -q` passes, including the concurrency single-call assertion.

### TASK-007: Config / lifecycle / cross-loop regression tests (FEAT-001)

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `tests/test_config_lifecycle.py` and `tests/test_execution_core.py`: `resolve_executor` valid/invalid; thread-safe single pool creation under concurrent first access; `set_thread_pool`/`set_process_pool` type validation (`UnblockError`); `shutdown()` idempotency and use-after-shutdown behavior; `atexit` registration; **cross-loop regression** — running asyncified work under a freshly created loop (and after a prior loop) does not raise "Future attached to a different loop" (plan correctness item #1); `in_worker_process()` is `False` in main process.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: (implementation is the tests).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: N/A.
- **Verify**: `pytest tests/test_config_lifecycle.py tests/test_execution_core.py -q` passes; cross-loop regression test passes.

### TASK-008: Coverage gate + cleanup

- [ ] **Implementation**: Configure `[tool.coverage.run]` (branch=true, source=`unblock`) and `[tool.coverage.report]` with `fail_under` set to the target (**95**); add appropriate `exclude_lines`. Wire `--cov=unblock --cov-branch --cov-fail-under=95` into the pytest invocation/CI. Ensure all legacy hollow tests are deleted and not counted.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: N/A (gate config).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Contributor note on the coverage gate and how to view the report.
- **Verify**: `pytest --cov=unblock --cov-branch --cov-report=term-missing` reports >=95% and exits 0; intentionally lowering coverage fails the gate.

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: Tests assert `UnblockError` on all documented misuse paths and exception propagation from workers.
- **Logging**: A test asserts the package attaches a `NullHandler` and does not leak logs (FEAT-001 policy).
- **Test isolation**: The autouse executor-reset fixture is mandatory — process-global pools otherwise bleed across tests and mask lifecycle bugs.
